InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.hksTitle: The Kuwait Program at Harvard Kennedy School (HKS)
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.hksIntro: Launched during the 2000–2001 academic year, today the Kuwait Program supports: residential fellowships for junior, mid-career, and senior scholars from or based in the State of Kuwait; a Customized Executive Education Program for leaders across Kuwait’s private, public, and nonprofit sectors.
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.hksAboutPara1: Launched in 2000 and generously supported by the Kuwait Foundation for the Advancement of Sciences (KFAS), the Kuwait Program at the John F. Kennedy School of Government at Harvard University aims to identify evidence-based, rigorously scientific solutions to public policy challenges facing the State of Kuwait, the Gulf region, and the world; provide professional development and advanced training to current and future leaders and scholars in Kuwait and the broader region; and facilitate exchanges of ideas, expertise, and know-how between Harvard and educational and policy-oriented institutions in the State of Kuwait and the region.
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.hksAboutPara2: Today, the Kuwait Program comprises the following program components:
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.hksBullet1: Residential fellowships at the Harvard Kennedy School for junior, mid-career, and senior scholars who are from or based in the State of Kuwait.
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.hksBullet2: Customized Executive Education Program for leaders across Kuwait's private, public, and nonprofit sectors.
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.hksBullet3: Collaborative research between Kuwait-based scholars and Harvard faculty.
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.hksBullet4: Research and internship opportunities, as well as on-campus events, for Harvard students to explore policy-relevant topics related to Kuwait.
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.ictpTitle: The Kuwait Program at The International Centre for Theoretical Physics (ICTP)
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.ictpIntro: The Kuwait Foundation for the Advancement of Sciences (KFAS) and ICTP have extended their longtime partnership for science advancement by creating a new joint programme of support for scientists from Kuwait and other Arab countries to be engaged in ICTP activities.
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.ictpOpportunitiesLabel: The Kuwait Programme at ICTP offers the following opportunities:
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.ictpOpp1Title: Supporting Scientists from Arab Countries to attend ICTP Scientific Calendar activities
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.ictpOpp1Body: ICTP organizes numerous conferences, schools and workshops every year through its Scientific Calendar of activities. The Kuwait Programme at ICTP offers support to Arab scientists who would like to attend an ICTP activity for a period not exceeding one month of their stay for the activity. Those who wish to apply for the fellowships should indicate so when using ICTP's online application system for Scientific Calendar activities. Choose an activity by viewing
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.ictpOpp2Body: ICTP's Postgraduate Diploma Programme is a 12-month pre-doctoral education programme for talented young science students from the Global South who have limited possibilities to pursue advanced studies in their home countries. The Kuwait Programme offers support for three Arab students to attend this intensive educational course selected on a competitive basis. Visit ICTP's
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.ictpOpp2Post: website for more details. The application deadline for the Programme is 28 February 2026. Applicants from Arab countries who are accepted into the Diploma Programme are considered for a KFAS grant.
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.ictpOpp3Body: Awarded to young Kuwaiti scientists who have completed their PhD and are working in Kuwait or abroad. The fellowship allows the scientists to continue their research in the areas of ICTP expertise, under the supervision of a resident scientist at the Centre. The fellow(s) will be selected by ICTP in consultation with KFAS. Selected fellows will be awarded a research fellowship agreement for a period of one year with the possibility to extend for an additional year.
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.ictpOpp4Title: Kuwait Visiting Scientists Scheme and Training and Research in Italian Laboratories (TRIL) Fellowships
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.ictpOpp4Body1: The visiting scientists scheme offers scientists and PhD students from Kuwait the opportunity to visit and collaborate with ICTP scientists for a period not exceeding 3 months per year. The purpose of the visits is to perform studies in ICTP's areas of expertise in close interaction with the Sections' resident staff as well as attending ongoing events (conferences, schools, seminars and workshops). The visiting scientists will be selected by ICTP in consultation with KFAS.
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.ictpOpp4Body2Pre: For longer stays, ICTP will make available its
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.ictpOpp4Body2Post: . The TRIL fellowship aims at allowing PhD students and scientists from Kuwait to undertake training and research in an Italian laboratory in different branches of the physical sciences. The fellows will be selected by ICTP in consultation with KFAS. The fellowship will be awarded once a year for a period not exceeding 12 months.
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.ictpApplyLabel: For more information about the Kuwait Programme at ICTP
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.ictpDeadlinePre: The deadline for applications to the Diploma Programme is
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.ictpEligibilityIntro: According to the agreement between KFAS and ICTP, postdoctoral fellowships are awarded exclusively to young Kuwaiti scientists. The only two programmes for which non-Kuwaiti scientists are eligible to apply are:
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.ictpEligibilityItem1: Participation in ICTP Scientific Calendar activities
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.lseTitle: The Kuwait Program at the London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE)
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.lseIntroPara1: The Kuwait Programme is a world-leading hub for research and expertise on Kuwait. The Programme is the main conduit through which research on Kuwait at the School is facilitated, expanded and promoted.
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.lseIntroPara2: The Programme is funded by the Kuwait Foundation for the Advancement of Sciences and directed by Professor Toby Dodge, who is also Kuwait Professor.
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.lseAim1: To establish collaborative research partnerships between LSE and Kuwaiti universities that will deliver high quality research on the challenges facing the country, expand Kuwaiti expertise on these challenges, and produce long-term relationships of value;
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.lseAim2: To conduct time-sensitive, policy-relevant research which will inform evidence-based policymaking in Kuwait and the wider GCC;
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.lseAim3: To produce and publish high quality original academic research on contemporary Kuwait;
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.lseAim4: To organize exchanges between LSE faculty and Kuwaiti academics and policy practitioners to facilitate knowledge exchange, intellectual capacity and career development;
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.lseAim5: To provide for the development of leaders and academics in Kuwaiti research and higher education institutions through the production, management and dissemination of research as well as through longer-term research collaboration activities.
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.lseTheme1: Improving the delivery of healthcare in Kuwait.
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.lseTheme2: Enhancing Kuwait's water and food security;
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.lseTheme3: Diversifying energy sources in an oil-rich economy;
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.lseTheme5: Developing public policy to reform the public sector and improve public service delivery in Kuwait;
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.lseTheme6: Developing economic and legal perspectives on diversifying the oil-dependent economy of Kuwait;
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.lseTheme7: Informing and improving environmental policy in Kuwait;
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.lseTheme8: Improving the role of the sovereign wealth fund in driving forward Kuwaiti development;
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.lseTheme9: Setting economic vision(s) to diversify the economy;
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.lseTheme10: Improving and developing education policies in Kuwait.
InternationalCollaborativeResearchPage.lseContactPre: For more information about the Programme, please contact

overviewParagraphs: KFAS empowers professionals, executives, and emerging leaders through world-class learning opportunities designed to strengthen leadership, innovation, and digital transformation capabilities.

Through partnerships with leading international universities and business schools, participants gain access to executive education, leadership development programs, innovation challenges, and specialized training in emerging technologies and strategic management. These opportunities equip individuals with the knowledge, skills, and global perspectives needed to drive organizational excellence, foster innovation, and contribute to Kuwait's transition toward a knowledge-based economy.
ProfessionalsPage.card1Body: KFAS offer seats on selected topics (in line with KFAS strategic direction) in courses by subject matter expert practitioners. This activity would focus on developing specific skills needed for organizational development. Short-courses (up to 5 days) are offered to all employee levels in open enrollment style. Courses are delivered with latest and highest standard of learning delivery. Seats are open to all Kuwaiti citizens, targeting the entire workforce population.
ProfessionalsPage.card2Body: KFAS offer grants to enhance the capabilities of the Kuwaiti human capital and sharpen their professional skills by a scheme to encourage individuals to obtain their professional credentials. An attractive monetary reward is given upon successfully obtaining the professional certificate. The amount of the reward will be determined based on KFAS policies and procedures for rewards.
ProfessionalsPage.localCoursesBody: KFAS bring executive education short-courses offered by international academic institutions on selected topics (in line with KFAS strategic direction) to Kuwait to offer seats locally and would be available to all Kuwaiti citizens.
ProfessionalsPage.abroadCoursesBody: KFAS offer seats on selected topics (in line with KFAS strategic direction) in programs already offered by academic institutions. This would allow participants to interact and exchange knowledge with other participants from around the world.
ProfessionalsPage.customizedBody: KFAS partners with academic institutions to create customized programs specifically for participants from Kuwait, offering blended and experiential learning experiences taught over a period of months.
ProfessionalsPage.innovationChallengeBody: One of our standout executive education programs is the annual KFAS Innovation Challenge, in which a small group of selected companies work with prestigious business schools to develop new initiatives and projects that advance a culture of innovation. After a competitive application process, executives and business leaders spend three to four months in structured learning and training, tackling a company-specific challenge.
ProfessionalsPage.harvardBody: A custom executive education program targeting the needs of the Kuwait private sector. Led by Professor Kessely Hong, the Harvard Kennedy School faculty team designed an impactful curriculum tailored to address the challenges and opportunities presented to managers — equipping them with collaborative and innovative tools for today's reality.
ProfessionalsPage.highPotentialBody: The vitality of every organization is dependent on the strength of its future leaders. By identifying high potential (Hi-Po) leadership candidates early and supporting their development, organizations drive significant returns on their human capital investments — maximizing strategic initiative, market competitiveness, and overall growth.

YouthPage.overviewPara1: KFAS provides learning opportunities targeting K-12 students, university undergraduate students, and educators. It emphasizes practical, interactive, and inclusive approaches to foster curiosity, scientific literacy, and innovation.
YouthPage.overviewPara2: Through STEAM “Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Mathematics” events and activities, this offering aims to drive and encourage, capacity building and knowledge transfer as well as outreach and networking for innovation ecosystem growth.
YouthPage.scopeGroup1Title: STEAM Capacity Building for Students & Educators
YouthPage.scopeGroup1Item1: Offer local and international learning and development opportunities for students and educators.
YouthPage.scopeGroup2Item1: Production of Sci & Tech media (videos, podcasts, and infographics) including collaboration with journalists and influencers to make science engaging and accessible.
YouthPage.scopeGroup2Item2: Offer mobile platform for delivering hands-on STEAM learning across Kuwait.
YouthPage.scopeGroup2Item3: Curation of events and activities under Science Month.
YouthPage.scopeGroup3Item1: Provide opportunities to engage in projects related to biodiversity, climate change, astronomy, and health.
YouthPage.scopeGroup3Item2: Develop online open-data platforms for public view and contributions.
YouthPage.scopeGroup3Item3: Establish recognition programs including badges, awards, and certificates.
YouthPage.ctaTitle: Apply now to participate in KFAS opportunities for the Youth.
YouthPage.genScienceBody1: Generation Science summer program empowers students in grades 7 to 12 to become ambassadors of STEM awareness and innovation within their schools and communities. By placing youth at the forefront of science and technology engagement, the program sparks a passion for discovery and motivates students to pursue STEM education and careers, ultimately contributing to building a strong, future-ready workforce. Student leaders represent their schools as the voice of STEM. They join a dynamic national cohort of young changemakers, receiving targeted leadership training, collaborating on STEM initiatives, and providing meaningful input to educators, industry professionals, and decision-makers across educational institutions, industry, and government.
YouthPage.genScienceGoal1: Develop students' leadership and communication skills.
YouthPage.genScienceGoal2: Strengthen interest in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM).
YouthPage.genScienceGoal3: Encourage students to actively participate in scientific and technical initiatives.
YouthPage.genScienceGoal4: Build a national network of young leaders passionate about innovation and science.
YouthPage.genScienceGoal5: Enable students to help shape the educational and scientific environment by voicing their ideas and recommendations to relevant stakeholders.
YouthPage.genScienceGoal6: Prepare a generation capable of contributing to a knowledge- and innovation-based economy.
YouthPage.scienceMonthBody1: Science Month is a national initiative that celebrates science, technology, and innovation through a diverse program of events and activities across Kuwait. The initiative brings together students, educators, researchers, and the wider community to explore the role of science in everyday life. Through workshops, exhibitions, talks, and interactive experiences, Science Month aims to inspire curiosity, promote scientific thinking, and encourage youth to pursue STEM fields. By fostering collaboration among local and international partners, the initiative contributes to building a vibrant science culture and supporting Kuwait's knowledge-based future.
YouthPage.scienceMonthGoal1: Promote scientific and technological literacy across all segments of society.
YouthPage.scienceMonthGoal2: Encourage students and young people to take an interest in STEM fields.
YouthPage.scienceMonthGoal3: Provide interactive learning experiences that connect science to everyday life.
YouthPage.scienceMonthGoal4: Highlight local and global scientific and creative achievements.
YouthPage.scienceMonthGoal5: Build effective partnerships between educational and research institutions and the public and private sectors.
YouthPage.scienceMonthGoal6: Contribute to preparing a generation capable of innovating and competing in the knowledge economy.
YouthPage.scienceMonthClosing: Through this initiative, KFAS continues to champion a culture of science and innovation, reinforcing Kuwait's position as a hub for knowledge and scientific creativity.
YouthPage.scienceBusBody1: The Science Bus brings interactive science experiences directly to schools and communities across Kuwait. Designed as a mobile learning platform, the Science Bus delivers engaging, hands-on activities that make science accessible, fun, and relevant to everyday life. Through immersive demonstrations and guided experiments, the program aims to spark curiosity, inspire young minds, and promote interest in STEM fields. By reaching diverse audiences nationwide, the Science Bus plays a key role in expanding access to quality science education and fostering a culture of discovery.
YouthPage.scienceBusGoal1: Promote scientific literacy among students and the wider community.
YouthPage.scienceBusGoal2: Deliver hands-on, interactive learning experiences in STEM fields.
YouthPage.scienceBusGoal3: Spark scientific curiosity and encourage exploration and experiential learning.
YouthPage.scienceBusGoal4: Raise awareness of the importance of science and technology in everyday life.
YouthPage.scienceBusGoal5: Reach schools and communities across all regions of Kuwait.
YouthPage.scienceBusGoal6: Support equal access to enjoyable, high-quality science education.
YouthPage.scienceBusGoal7: Help instill a culture of innovation and discovery among younger generations.
YouthPage.scienceBusClosing: Through this initiative, the Science Bus brings science closer to the community and turns learning into an inspiring, interactive experience, supporting KFAS's vision of spreading scientific knowledge and nurturing youth interest in innovation.

overviewParagraphs: KFAS provides grants to support impactful events and activities that promote science, technology, and innovation across Kuwait. This offering aims to engage the community, inspire youth, and strengthen the culture of scientific thinking through interactive and educational experiences.
ActivitiesEventsSponsorshipPage.objective1Body: Promote public understanding of science and technology
ActivitiesEventsSponsorshipPage.objective2Body: Encourage youth engagement in STEM fields
ActivitiesEventsSponsorshipPage.objective3Body: Support knowledge sharing and community outreach
ActivitiesEventsSponsorshipPage.objective4Body: Foster collaboration between institutions and experts
ActivitiesEventsSponsorshipPage.citizenScienceBody1: Citizen Science Grant supports initiatives that directly involve the public in scientific research and data collection, with the aim of broadening access to science, strengthening community participation, and enhancing science literacy.
ActivitiesEventsSponsorshipPage.citizenScienceBody2: Through participatory research models, citizen science initiatives enable non-specialist participants to contribute meaningfully to scientific inquiry under appropriate scientific guidance. Supported initiatives are expected to generate reliable data, address locally relevant challenges, and contribute to evidence-based knowledge that benefits the State of Kuwait.
ActivitiesEventsSponsorshipPage.citizenScienceBody3: Citizen science initiatives may be implemented through field activities, digital platforms, community-based monitoring, or other participatory approaches, provided that scientific rigor, and ethical standards.
scienceCommParagraphs: Science Communication Grant supports initiatives that disseminate scientific knowledge, simplify complex scientific concepts, and enhance public understanding and appreciation of science in everyday life. Also initiatives that translate scientific and technological knowledge into accessible, engaging, and meaningful content for non-specialist audiences. This offering aims to strengthen science culture, promote informed public discourse, and increase awareness of scientific and technological developments across society.
ActivitiesEventsSponsorshipPage.scopeIntro: KFAS supports initiatives that bring science closer to society and create meaningful impact in people's lives. Areas of support include projects and initiatives that help spread scientific knowledge and strengthen the culture of science in Kuwait.
ActivitiesEventsSponsorshipPage.scopeItem1Body: (such as books, magazines, journals, and periodicals), provided that the application is submitted by an academic institution, research center, or professional public-benefit association.
ActivitiesEventsSponsorshipPage.scopeItem2Body: including visual, audio, and digital media (e.g. documentaries, videos, podcasts, digital series, and campaigns) with scientific or technological content aimed at disseminating scientific knowledge and raising public awareness of technological advancements.
ActivitiesEventsSponsorshipPage.scopeItem3Body: including exhibitions, festivals, and public awareness activities with scientific and technological content, targeting:
ActivitiesEventsSponsorshipPage.eligibilityItem2: Non-profit organizations and civil society groups
ActivitiesEventsSponsorshipPage.eligibilityItem4: Relevant public and private sector organizations
ActivitiesEventsSponsorshipPage.ctaTitle: Apply now and bring your initiative to life with KFAS support.

This centralized resource is designed to ensure consistent and accurate representation of the Kuwait Foundation for the Advancement of Sciences brand across all platforms. Here, you will find our official brand guidelines, primary and secondary color palettes, and specific rules for correct logo usage. Please review our visual identity requirements and download the official asset packs and comprehensive PDF guideline manuals below to ensure your materials align with our standards.
